programme.title: Jaan-E-Mann: Let's Fall in Love... Again
programme.desc: To avoid paying a hefty alimony, Suhaan (Salman Khan) thinks of getting his ex-wife Pia (Preity Zinta) remarried. For that, he convinces Pia¡¯s simpleton college friend Agastya Rao (Akshay Kumar) that he can still marry his college crush. He then helps Agastya to woo Pia, but ends up falling in love with her all over again.

The simple job turns into protecting their homeland against neighboring country's mafia to use their land to expand their dirty business.

programme.title: Anonymous
programme.desc: Artemis and Cash set out to take down a child sex trafficking ring and, in doing so discover Artemis' long lost son, only to find there are grave consequences that may cost them their lives and the ones they love. Unbeknownst to Artemis, the hard core enforcer, Olivier (Ernest L. Thomas -What's Happening, Malcolm X, Everybody Hates Chris) is also searching for the boy and willing to do anything to find him.

programme.desc: Despite multiple pressures, a shocking discovery causes NASA to cancel a historic launch.

programme.desc: Jim investigates a strange set of laws that link together everything from engines to humans to stars.

programme.title: The Story of Information
programme.desc: Jim learns that information isn't just about human communication it's woven into the very fabric of reality.

programme.title: Great African Grasslands: Savannah
programme.desc: African savannah regions support a much greater diversity of wildlife than temperate grasslands.

programme.title: Water Planet: Open Ocean
programme.desc: Underwater creatures must make homes in the deep wherever survival can be assured.

programme.title: Deserts
programme.desc: The desert is an extreme environment where wildlife must adapt to survive. Many animals are nocturnal.

programme.title: Amazon Rainforest
programme.desc: Rainforests are known as the lungs of our planet. And the Amazon rainforest is the heart of Brazil.

programme.title: Masters of the Sky
programme.desc: The masters of the sky are the winged predators of the aerial world. They have evolved as master predators.

programme.title: Arctic
programme.desc: The Arctic is alive with a fascinating assortment of wildlife species, hardy breeds uniquely suited to cold.

programme.title: Iron Island
programme.desc: Some poor people in the Southern coasts of Iran do not have any place to live, and thus, they reside on an old, abandoned ship in the sea. Captain Nemat, their chief, tries to persuade the ship-owner and the official authorities not to get the ship back. On the other hand, he is selling the iron parts of the ship piece by piece. “Iron Island” is the story of those whose ignorance makes them simply trust those who are always ready to abuse their trust.

programme.title: Turning Green
programme.desc: Set in late 1970s Ireland, it tells the story of 16-year-old James Powers, an American who finds himself lost after his mother dies and he is forced to live with his three Irish aunts. Displaced and depressed, he longs for a way to make it back to America. One lucky weekend in London, James discovers pornography and, desperate for cash, he decides to sell them back in Ireland. His success spreads wildly. After finding a possible way home, he must decide where home really is, and finds that one's place in the world is all a state of mind.

programme.title: Beyond The Trophy
programme.desc: TROPHY tells the story of men's obsession for power and how far they will go sometimes claim the "Prize". TROPHY adds a new twist to the stereotypical mob movies in that the TROPHY woman gets more than the usual benefits of rolling with the Bad Boys, she gets Mr. Right. TROPHY has twists, turns, showdowns, seduction, humor, the power of the Mafia and the reality that dreams really do come true.

programme.title: L'Estraneo
programme.desc: Cristian falls in love with Greta, a girl speaking with Spanish accent, a little bit younger than him, who spreads a light and enthusiasm quite different from Cristian's. He lives his love affairs, but, as often happens, “love is not enough”, and soon Christian himself calls into question his whole life and people belonging to it, in order to find serenity.

programme.title: Litorai
programme.desc: A short documentary that tells the story of a town, a marriage and a way of understanding life that only remains in memories.

programme.title: Keepsake
programme.desc: Janine is a young woman on the road to a new life when a freak accident strands her on a deserted highway and a call for help soon becomes a fight for survival. Stalked and tormented by a psychotic tow truck driver, Janine soon finds herself trapped in a world of torture and depravity, imprisoned in a cellar and given only thirty days to live! Janine must contend with both the ghosts of her past and the demons of her present in an effort to survive.

programme.title: Faraar Kab Tak
programme.desc: The Horrifying Fact That Millions Of Unidentified Criminals Are Absconding Across The Nation, Is Enough To Frighten Anyone! The Show Is A Clutter Breaking Factual Crime Drama Series That Appeals To The Inherent Security Concern Of A Family. Engaging Police Procedure On True Crime Cases Is Brought To Life With An Edge Of Seat Narrative.

programme.title: Humkadam
programme.desc: Humkadam is a first of its kind show on Indian television that presents the complicated dynamics of ‘Saas' and ‘Bahu' in a clutter breaking manner. It tells the story of Tara and Shanti who are two diametrically opposite personalities, stuck in unprecedented situations. Just when they thought life had come to a full stop, is where they had to put their differences aside and start a new journey. Will they become each other's Humkadam to face all the adversities together?

programme.title: Dhartiputra Nandini
programme.desc: Dhartiputra Nandini Tells The Story Of A Farmer Girl Who Wants To Stay Connected To Her Land. At The Same Time, Akash Returns From America. Sumitra Devi Wishes That His Grandson Akash Should Take Over The Entire Business By Becoming “Ceo.” At The Same Time, Her Family Is Falling Apart, And She Needs An Ideal Daughter-In-Law Who Understands The Responsibility Of Being Her Daughter-In-Law. She Sees Nandini As That, But Akash Refuses To Accept Her Since She Is Not Educated. Nandini Proves That She Is The Best, Both As A Supporter And A Life Partner. She Is There For Akash'S Family In Every Circumstance. This Is The Journey Of “Dhartiputra Nandini” From “Life Partner For Life” To “Charioteer.” In Addition, She Manages His Business. Nandini Also Fulfills Akash'S Dreams And Makes The Name Of The Country Known To The Entire World

programme.title: Laal Banarasi
programme.desc: The Story Highlights The Exploitation Happening With The ‘Bunkars' In Banaras, Whose Craft Is Appreciated All Around The World, But They Are Paid Peanuts By The Traders. Amidst This, Blooms A Love Story Between The Two Opposites – Gauri, A Bunkar And Garv, A Trader. The Story Is Of Gauri Who Belongs To The Bunkar Community Facing A Series Of Incidents That Destroy Her Family And Swears To Bring About A Change In Their Life And Stop The Exploitation By Directly Trading The Sarees. This Becomes A Battle With Traders Creating Hurdles In Her Life But She Is Unstoppable.

programme.title: Mid
programme.desc: Veteran actor, Naseeruddin Shah, explores the many untold stories of Indian cricket. He recalls the history of the game match highlights. Sportsmanship, stadiums, hits and misses, and many more. In his unique narrative style, Naseeruddin Shah brings alive the humorous side of the game, along with undocumented anecdotes and quirky trivia. The show also features many personalities who narrate cricketing experiences and stories including Shashi Tharoor, Narl Contractor, Bishan Singh Bedi and Charu Sharma amongst others

programme.title: The Way Back Home
programme.desc: Rohan Thakur finds his true calling as he begins a journey to revisit his roots in Himachal Pradesh. Way Back Home is as much a travelogue of childhood, as of growing up. Get ready for a journey of a lifetime

programme.title: Regiment Diaries
programme.desc: The Indian Army is an emotion – a living chronicle of events, that we have the luxury to forget because they remember. The army itself is a family of several regiments, each of which has its own history, identity, and glorious traditions. Regiment Diaries is a tale of the Indian Army unlike any told before. Told by the very men who follow the illustrious feats of their predecessors, it tells timeless tales that warm the heart, moisten the eye, and leave you in awe of the bravest children of India.

programme.title: Tyohaar Ki Thaali
programme.desc: Sakshi Tanwar in Tyohaar Ki Thaali decides to celebrate one festival a week and along with the celebration, she also prepares two dishes from her mom's diary which is her prized possession. Along with preparing the dishes she also reminisces into the moments of how she celebrated some festivals and also shares some stories about the Gods.

programme.title: Destruction Decoded
programme.desc: This series will go back through the record to explore life-altering, earth-shattering, unforgettable events using a combination of carefully selected archive, cutting edge CGI and informative scripting in this popular science format. As well as exploring spectacular archive, illustrating the full toll these disasters can take, we'll dig into the science behind each event, shining a light on the most fascinating elements and giving an understanding on how and why these events occur

programme.title: Ekaant
programme.desc: India is known for its remarkable architecture and serene landscapes. Its rich history is steeped in mystery its vastness - lends itself to several stories and urban legends. Ekaant takes you through the abandoned roads and alleys of some of India's historic locations. Travelling through the spread out palaces and deserted forts, the show documents and investigates the eerie processes of abandonment of each site. How flourishing towns and cities came to be in ruins, is the question each episode seeks to answer. Ekaant explores the ceasing of life and the triumph of nature even in the most populated of places and takes you on the road no longer travelled.

programme.title: Mid
programme.desc: Veteran actor, Naseeruddin Shah, explores the many untold stories of Indian cricket. He recalls the history of the game match highlights. Sportsmanship, stadiums, hits and misses, and many more. In his unique narrative style, Naseeruddin Shah brings alive the humorous side of the game, along with undocumented anecdotes and quirky trivia. The show also features many personalities who narrate cricketing experiences and stories including Shashi Tharoor, Narl Contractor, Bishan Singh Bedi and Charu Sharma amongst others

programme.desc: Giant jellyfish are preparing to attack the coastal towns of Japan. The giant Nomura jellyfish grows to over 2m long and up to 200kg. Rising sea temperatures have seen an increase in these monsters, and this year will see the biggest attack ever.

programme.title: Super Senses
programme.desc: On this episode we travel from the desert, to the forest, Australia to Borneo and back to learn how species as diverse as Bat-eared Foxes, the Bilby, Deer, Kangaroo Rats, cicadas, Proboscis Monkeys and other creatures never miss an auditory trick.

programme.title: Super Senses
programme.desc: On this episode of Animal Super Senses, we get in touch with the feelings of wild animals. Really in touch. Because animals, just like humans, rely heavily on their sense of touch, every day in every way.

programme.title: Super Senses
programme.desc: On this episode of Animal Super Senses, we examine how things taste, and how vital scent is from the animal perspective. But their sense of smell will also detect the scent of odorants carried through the air.

programme.title: Super Senses
programme.desc: There are senses we share with most animals, but what about those senses that human is equipped with? Infrared light, electromagnetic waves and ultrasound, are all found within a spectrum of sensory information that just passes by ordinary humans.

programme.title: One World Kitchen
programme.desc: Bring on the seafood! Home cook Mary Tang kicks off this episode of One World Kitchen 3 with a unique dish featuring chewy clams, sweet and bitter fermented black beans, and a collection of spices. Next, she makes us her favourite shrimp dish - Shrimp and Egg Fried Rice. Fluffy egg bits, lightly flavoured nutty brown rice, and a pop of tender shrimp works wonders for the taste buds. A common appetizer, Mary then walks us through a spicy concoction of deep-fried salt and pepper squid or Calamari. And who doesn't love lobster? Mary finishes off with Cantonese Lobster with Ginger and Scallions (yes, it tastes as good as it sounds!). A Cantonese take on the juicy protein, this dish is fried in hot bubbling oil for a golden-brown crust, and steamed in great Shaoxing wine and salty soy sauce for some flavour. (S3 E06)

programme.title: One World Kitchen
programme.desc: A tasty four-course Vietnamese meal using fruit? Yes, please! For her first dish, talented home cook Lisa Nguyen uses tangy tamarind pulp to balance flavours in a savoury bowl of Vietnamese Sour Fish Soup. Then, she combines tart pomelo and sweet pineapple with juicy crab meat for a flavourful salad. Next up, hot Chicken Coconut Curry with a hint of citrusy lemongrass, sweet potatoes, and a kaffir lime leaf. Pair it with a warm, toasty baguette and you're good to go! Lisa's last dish is a mouth-watering, classic Vietnamese dessert. Soft and chewy Steamed Banana Cake drizzled in a sweet velvety coconut sauce crunchy sesame seeds will have you begging for more! (S3 E13)

programme.title: One World Kitchen
programme.desc: Talented home cook Jasmin Ibrahim prepares delicious, sharable small plates in this episode of One World Kitchen. First on the menu, Grilled Lamb Skewers. Wonderfully charred and seasoned with sweet cinnamon, potent all-spice, and a punch of garlic, these lamb skewers will be the hype at your next family get-together! Then, Jasmin makes a famous Lebanese classic - Falafel and Hummus (so tasty!). Next up, savoury Zucchini Fritters with a zesty yogurt sauce are added to the mix for a little dinner party pizzazz. Finally, Jasmin's favourite cheese is the star of this next dish - Grilled Halloumi. Served with a sweet and citrusy rose drink, this small plate will have your guests going crazy! (S3 E20)

programme.title: One World Kitchen
programme.desc: Cheese - a staple in traditional Greek cuisine! Talented home cook Joanna Chery fries up two delicious ap- petizers called Saganaki, both featuring two different types of cheese. First, she fries up kefaltyri, a hard, salty cheese in a dramatic burning flame of ouzo (licorice-flavoured alcohol) - Opa! Then, Joanna shows us a popular Fried Shrimp and Cheese appetizer using tasty feta and tender shrimp. Pair both dishes with a Greek rustic bread and you've nailed it! Next up, Joanna prepares a Greek classic - Spanakopita. This mouth-watering dish is made up of creamy ricotta, crispy phyllo pastry, fresh spinach, and a hint of dill (yum!). Moving on, Joanna showcases her all-time favourite Greek meal - Pastisio. For dessert, cheesy sweet tarts called Melitinia Cookies. Commonly served at parties, these ooey-gooey mascarpone-filled creations are to die for! (S3 E27)

programme.title: Tauric Chersonesos, Crimea
programme.desc: 528 BC. in the west of the Heracles Peninsula, the Dorian Greeks founded the city of Chersonesus in Crimea, which means "peninsula". Founded as a colony of the wealthy Greek city of Heraclea of Pontus, its history is part of the Ancient Greece, Ancient Rome and Byzantium. In the Middle Ages, Chersonesus was called Kherson.

programme.title: Palazzo Ducale di Urbino, Italy
programme.desc: Hidden in the hills of Italy lies Palazzo Ducale di Urbino, the Ducal Palace of Urbino. It may seem strange to find such grand Renaissance architecture outside of the great cities. We all know about Rome, Venice, Florence and their great works. But the Ducal Palace of Urbino is often forgotten, despite rivaling all of them with its splendor and great history.

programme.title: Kursi, National Park, Israel
programme.desc: We are 30km west of Jerusalem, on the border between the Judean Mountains and the Ayalon Valley, in the village of Nicopolis. You may know it better as Emmaus. Here, thanks to its strategic position, Emmaus has played an important role in the country's administrative, military and economic life many times in its history.

programme.title: Marmashen Monastery, Armenia
programme.desc: Here we are: in a beautiful valley on the left bank of Akhurian River, 10 km northwest of the city of Gyumri, 2 km southwest of the village of Vahramaberd in the Shirak region in Armenia. We are at Marmashen Monastery. Surrounded by fruit orchards this magnificent monument once was a prominent cultural and religious center and is considered as the finest example of the medieval architecture of Armenia…

programme.title: San Nicolo dei Mendicoli, Italy
programme.desc: Venice, the dream city in the north of Italy! It is located on 118 islands in the Venetian lagoon, separated by 150 canals and channels with about 400 bridges. Known as the Floating City or the City of Canals, Venice is a true monument city. The church of"&" San Nicolo dei Mendicoli is a historical and artistic achievement, inseparable from the Venetian heritage.

programme.title: Punta Del Este, Uruguay
programme.desc: With sprawling beaches, iconic monuments and a unique mix of colonial and modern architecture, it is the jewel of Uruguay's southern coast. Often called the “Pearl of the Atlantic”, likened with Miami and Cannes, it is the city of Punta del Este.

programme.title: Voronezh town, Russia
programme.desc: In the southern part of Russia, by the river Voronezh, there is a city with the same name. It has a long history of remarkable men and women who prospered and fell here. The city fought for freedom countless times and brought a lot of talented artists to the world. It is a city of strong people and fantastic architecture. Voronezh was founded in 1585 when tsar Fyodor ordered to start the building of a new fortress on the Voronezh-river's bank to create a barrier from southern invasions. The fortress was finished around 1586 and defended Russians only for four years before it was burnt to the ground by cossacks.

programme.title: The Trinity Cathedral, Saint Petersburg, Russia
programme.desc: Spread out on the coast of the Gulf of Finland, at the mouth of the Neva River, St. Petersburg is the second-largest city in Russia. Witness of both military and civil history, the Trinity Cathedral of Izmailovsky, part of the historic center of St. Peter"&"sburg is a world heritage site.

programme.title: Novi Sad Synagogue, Serbia
programme.desc: Right in the city center of Novi Sad, Serbia, this crucial area, on Jevrejska Street – literally, Jewish Street - the synagogue of Novi Sad occupies a prominent spot both geographically as well as in the historical annals of the city.

programme.title: Mar Elias Monastery
programme.desc: Mar Elias monastery lies on the side of the road from Jerusalem and Bethlehem. It is maintained by the Greek-Orthodox church, and by local tradition Elijah rested here while fleeing from Queen Jezebel

programme.title: City of Venice, Italy (Part 2)
programme.desc: Celebrated throughout the world for its singular beauty, discover Venice and some of its famous places. An open-air museum that seems to float on its lagoon, and a World Heritage Site.

programme.title: Kaleto Archaeological Complex & Vrachanski Balkan Nature Park, Bulgaria
programme.desc: Located in northwestern Bulgaria, in the Vratsa Province, Mezdra is a small, picturesque town sitting along the left bank of the Iskar River. Discover the historical heritage of a living city: the Kaleto Archaeological Complex and the Vrachanski Balkan Nature Park. They are containing karst landscapes and habitats with more than 600 caves.

programme.title: Ethno Village-Museum Ljubacke Doline, Bosnia & Herzegovina
programme.desc: Exploring different cultures and experiencing lives utterly different from yours is the best part of discovering new horizons. This kind of experience can be done in one particular place in Bosnia & Herzegovina called Ljubacke Doline. Well, you can translate it literally by Kisses Valley. This place is one of the many Ethno villages in the region.

programme.title: The Palace Museum (The Forbidden City), China (Part 1)
programme.desc: The Forbidden City, also known as the Palace Museum, is located in the center of Beijing. From 1368 to 1911, it was the Chinese imperial Palace of the Ming and Qing dynasties. It is a World Heritage Site as one of the best-preserved ancient wooden palace complexes. A documentary series in two episodes.

programme.title: Stamboli's Countryhouse, Crimea
programme.desc: Stamboli Villa is the most beautiful building in the city of Feodosia, located in the Crimean peninsula. It was built from 1909 to 1914.

programme.title: Tigre, the Puerto de Frutos, Argentina
programme.desc: The architectural ensemble of Tigre's rowing clubs is a World Heritage site. The unique factor was the pluralism of nationalities and cultural trends that existed in Argentina at the beginning of the 20th Century.

programme.title: Traveling With Denella Ri'chard
programme.desc: The Exumas, The Bahamas' first natural marine sea park. Swim with the pigs or the sharks – if you DARE! In this episode, travel expert Denella Ri'chard explores this pristine chain of islands in The Bahamas archipelago via boat and ATV.

programme.title: Traveling With Denella Ri'chard
programme.sub-title: Come to the Virgin Islands: St. Thomas
programme.desc: Meet Denella Ri'chard. Denella is a sought after world traveler, Reconnect with paradise in the U.S. Virgin Islands as Denella explores the beautiful islands of St. Thomas & St. Croix.

programme.title: Antalya, Turkey
programme.desc: When King Attalos first set eyes on Antalya he called it “Paradise on Earth”. Its fertile soil and natural harbour made it favourable for settlers throughout the ages. Therefore Antalya has a rich cultural heritage with many remains of an outstanding hist"&"ory. The Persians, Alexander the Great, the Romans, the Arabs and many more have left their mark on the area over the centuries but no one more than King Attalos of Pergamon who in 159 B.C. gave the region its name Attaleia, which leaded to the modern nam"&"e Antalya....

programme.title: Pskov City, Russia
programme.desc: Pskov: One of the most beautiful and ancient cities located in the north-west of Russia on the Velikaya River! Only 20 kilometers from the Estonian border, church-studded city Pskov is dominated by its mighty riverside Kremlin: an enormous bulwark that ha"&"s faced up to its fair share of invading armies down the centuries.

programme.title: Sassi di Matera, Italy
programme.desc: Between the toe and heel of Southern Italy, there is the Basilicata region. This mountainous and arid landscape of the area has ensured a relatively sparse population and only in recent years has local infrastructure been developed. One of the larger popu"&"lation centers in this remote area is the unique and spectacular City of Sassi di Matera. Matera is internationally famed for its Sassi areas which have initially been prehistoric troglodyte settlements. The cavernous habitations were painstakingly dug ou"&"t of the soft calcareous rock, which is a characteristic of this part of Southern Italy.

programme.title: HOTELS in Maldives Part 3
programme.desc: Would you prefer a prestigious palace, an eco-lodge in the middle of the jungle, a charming villa or a design temple in an urban setting? LUXURY DREAMS takes you on a voyage of discovery of rare addresses, always top luxury, often quite unusual. Today direction the Maldives (Part 3)

programme.desc: Jack meets a man who is unable to recognise faces and puts a super-recogniser to the test.

programme.title: Into America's Wild
programme.desc: Step off the beaten path and connect to your inner trailblazer in this cross-country journey to some of the most beautiful but little-known landscapes of North America. Into America's Wild is a non-stop ride via kayak, train, bike, hot air balloon and more that explores the transformational allure of wild places and the human connection we all share with the natural world.

programme.title: Wild Travels
programme.desc: Will finds himself in the middle of an ice cream catastrophe at Ben & Jerry's Factory in Waterbury, Vermont mingles with mermaids at the underwater show in Weeki Wachee, Florida achieves speeds of 5 miles per hour on the cog wheel train to the summit of Mt. Washington, New Hampshire buys new kicks at a Sneaker convention in Cleveland and marvels at Bug Art portraits at a museum in St. Johnsbury, Vermont.

programme.title: Wild Travels
programme.desc: Will tries and fails to ride an alligator at Orlando's Gatorland drops in on the only used car dealership with a priceless collection of Muhammad Ali memorabilia in New Hampshire dabbles in the paranormal at the abandoned and haunted West Virginia State Penitentiary and then meets an outrageous artist who is creating a psychedelic city block in Pittsburgh known as “Randyland.”

programme.title: Wild Travels
programme.desc: Will learns the hazards and rewards of professional Golf Ball Diving in Kissimmee, Florida considers defecting to a foreign country at a library that sits smack dab on the Vermont – Canadian border discovers a Hari Krishna commune with an incredible “Palace of Gold” in the hills of West Virginia marvels at outdoor sculptures at the African-American Heritage Museum in Hammond, Louisiana, and catches his breath in Donora, Pennsylvania at the world's only “Smog Museum.”

programme.title: Wild Travels
programme.desc: Will meets Mr. Pierogi, Ms. Pasczki and the polka-loving Pierorgettes at Pierogi Fest in Whiting, Indiana ducks for cover at Lambert's Café in Alabama discovers where the bullets are buried in Tombstone Arizona's Bird Cage theater is shocked by the products made from endangered animals confiscated by the National Wildlife Property Repository near Denver and cheers on the daring (and possibly deranged) participants at a Unicycle Football game in San Marcos, Texas.

programme.title: Q: Alabama's Barbecue Legends
programme.desc: The story of Alabama barbecue can't be told in sentences. It's told through whiffs of smoke, buckets of sauce and friendly nods across the counter. See how a simple dish has become a culinary anthem for Alabama.

programme.title: Love On the Rocks
programme.desc: A romantic trip to Colorado takes a windy turn when Jason pushes Tayler, despite her hesitation, to summit Mt. Democrat in 70 mph winds and -19 degree weather. (S1 E01)

programme.title: Love On the Rocks
programme.desc: Jason loves mountain biking. Tayler prefers to read a book. Jason is up for any adventure tossed at him. Tayler likes to be a bit more prepared. Will the adventures that await in Arkansas leave the couple questioning if they have anything in common? (S1 E02)
